featureName: BUG-073-002: web assistant client had no single-flight guard, no request timeout, and no busy affordance

executionHistory.summary: Sweep round 15 (chaos lens) of spec 073 found web/pwa/assistant.js dispatches turns with no single-flight guard (rapid double-submit -> overlapping turns with distinct transport_message_ids), fetches with no AbortController (hung endpoint freezes the composer), and never disables the Send button. Mobile Dart client audited clean.

Single bugfix-fastlane scope: inFlight single-flight guard + AbortController timeout + setComposerBusy affordance, locked by a Go source-contract guard (the page is auth-gated; browser interaction tests cannot reach the composer). SCN-073-ROBUST-01..03 authored.

web/pwa/assistant.js: added inFlight flag + TURN_TIMEOUT_MS; dispatchTurn early-returns when inFlight and toggles setComposerBusy; postTurn builds an AbortController, passes signal to fetch, aborts after TURN_TIMEOUT_MS, surfaces a timeout error; the submit handler early-returns when inFlight without clearing the composer.

Added web/pwa/tests/assistant_robustness_guard_test.go: a source-contract guard asserting AbortController + signal + inFlight (>=2) + .disabled=busy + TURN_TIMEOUT_MS (>=2), with an adversarial twin proving a regressed source fails every contract. Both PASS; re-RED proven by stripping the wiring (FAIL naming the missing mechanisms). node --check clean.
Full web/pwa/tests Go package green (ok 1.377s): storage guard + codegen-drift guard + new robustness guard. No forbidden storage API introduced.
One flag + one helper + one AbortController; the inFlight guard covers all dispatch entry points (submit/confirm/disambiguation/retry) with no duplication.
AbortController bounds the request; finally clears the timer and the busy state on every path. No flakiness surface.
Same-origin HttpOnly cookie auth unchanged; no storage/XSS regression (storage guard green). Single-flight also reduces accidental duplicate-turn submission. No framework files touched.
artifact-lint PASSED for the packet; node --check clean; full web/pwa/tests package green. Evidence in report.md.
Diff confined to web/pwa/assistant.js + the new guard test; no migration; no .github/bubbles changes; parent spec 073 artifacts and the mobile client untouched.
assistant.js comments document the single-flight contract, the timeout budget, and the busy affordance; the guard test documents the auth-gating rationale.

scopeProgress.title: Single-flight, time-bounded, busy-aware web assistant client

lockdownState.reason: bugfix-fastlane client robustness fix; no convergence loop required

resolvedFindings.summary: web/pwa/assistant.js had a submit race (overlapping turns with distinct transport_message_ids), no fetch timeout (hung endpoint froze the composer), and no busy affordance.
resolvedFindings.resolution: Added an inFlight single-flight guard (dispatchTurn + submit handler), an AbortController request timeout (TURN_TIMEOUT_MS) in postTurn, and a setComposerBusy disabled-Send affordance. Locked by a Go source-contract guard with an adversarial twin (the auth-gated page blocks a browser interaction test).
resolvedFindings.evidence: report.md Test/Validation Evidence; web/pwa/assistant.js diff; web/pwa/tests/assistant_robustness_guard_test.go; full web/pwa/tests package ok 1.377s.
